Closure description:
Parameters adjusted on ADFS as in the TEST environment.

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

Notes:
26.11.24 Ticket opened by Employee 16.
26.11.24 Ticket assigned

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?

Activities
03.06.25 07:45
Phone report.
Issue:
External users can no longer log in to the customer portal.
URL:
https://portal.kf40.ch
Browser error message:
502 – Bad Gateway
Internally (via internal network) login works.
Priority: High (customers affected)
03.06.25 07:52
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 29
Status: In Progress
Notes
03.06.25 08:30
Helpdesk checks:
• External DNS resolution correct
• Certificate valid
• HTTPS reachable
• Internal backend (app server) reachable
Test:
External → 502
Internal → works
Suspicion: Reverse Proxy / WAF
03.06.25 09:10
Customer adds:
A new certificate was installed over the weekend.
Additionally, there was a firewall update (security patch).
03.06.25 10:25
Helpdesk checks firewall:
• Rule for port 443 present
• NAT correct
• No blocking visible in logs
Load balancer status:
Backend server marked as “Healthy”.
03.06.25 11:40
Test with direct IP to Reverse Proxy → works.
Test via FQDN → 502
Notable:
Header “X-Forwarded-Proto” missing in external request.
04.06.25 09:05
Customer asks whether certificate may be incorrectly bound.
Certificate validation:
• CN correct
• SAN correct
• Chain complete
No obvious errors.
05.06.25 08:50
Ticket forwarded to Network / Security Team.
Assigned group changed to: BU Network & Security
06.06.25 09:30
Taken over by System Engineer 21
WAF analysis:
• Policy active
• TLS inspection active
• HTTP/2 enabled
Notable:
After patch, default profile was reloaded.
06.06.25 11:15
Test: TLS inspection temporarily disabled →
Login works.
Inspection re-enabled →
502 again.
09.06.25 10:05
Further analysis:
App server expects header:
X-Forwarded-Proto: https
When TLS inspection is active, header is not forwarded.
09.06.25 14:40
Comparison with architecture documentation performed.
Reference in internal document:
“Blueprint: Secure Web Publishing – KF40 – Version 3.2”
Section 4.3 describes special header forwarding in case of TLS offloading.
10.06.25 08:25
Blueprint document loaded from DMS.
Relevant notes:
• TLS termination must occur on WAF
• Backend expects HTTPS signaling
• Custom header rewrite rule required
• Inspection policy must not remove HTTP headers
• Specific exception defined for portal application
10.06.25 11:45
Configuration comparison with blueprint shows:
• Custom header rewrite rule missing
• HTTP profile does not match documented version
• Inspection template “Default-Strict” active instead of “Portal-App-Template”
12.06.25
Internal coordination:
Firewall patch overwrote template.
Custom policy was not reassigned.
16.06.25
Configuration reviewed and adjusted according to blueprint.
No technical details documented in ticket (security policy).
18.06.25 14:55
External access test successful.
Multiple login attempts tested.
Monitoring stable for 24 hours.
Ticket closure
18.06.25 15:33
Dear Contact Person 19
The root cause was a deviation in the Reverse Proxy configuration from the documented architectural specification following a security patch.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal blueprint documentation.
The customer portal is now fully accessible again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

• Mobile devices (iOS) work
• Error occurs only when sending

→ Likely SMTP authentication or Hybrid Connector issue
14.01.25 09:15 – Client-side troubleshooting

14.01.25 10:30 – Exchange Admin Center analysis
• Checked Mailflow → Queue Monitoring
• No global Microsoft 365 outage reported
• Performed Message Trace → no blocking visible
• SMTP AUTH globally disabled? → No

14.01.25 11:12 – Escalation

14.01.25 11:16

15.01.25 08:55 – System Engineer 07
Hybrid setup analysis:
• Environment: Exchange Hybrid (OnPrem + M365)
• Send Connector checked
• TLS certificate checked
• SPF / DKIM unchanged
Log analysis on Exchange Server:
Event Log shows:
535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful
SMTP AUTH was disabled for the affected users.
15.01.25 10:20 – Root cause identified
Tenant security policy was adjusted last week:
Security Default → enabled
Result: SMTP AUTH disabled for legacy clients
Sales team still uses older Outlook version (Build 2019 MSI).
15.01.25 11:05 – Actions
• SMTP AUTH selectively enabled for affected users
• Conditional Access Policy adjusted
• Test sending successful
16.01.25 09:00 – Monitoring
Mailflow monitored for 24 hours
No further errors in logs

All users can send normally again.

Dear Contact Person 08
Your ticket dated 14.01.25 has been successfully closed.
Root cause:
SMTP AUTH was disabled due to new Security Default Policy.
Action taken:
Selective activation and adjustment of the Conditional Access Policy.
If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.
